MISS LAMONT IS HONOR


GUEST.
Miss Jean Lamont is being enter.
tanned this afternoon by the girls
of the Young Woman's Christian
association at the home of Mrs. J
X. Smith, on Short court. The
hours for the reception will be
from 2:30 to o'clock


4 4 4


MRS. MANN Is HOSTESS.
Mrs. S. A. Mann is entertaining
the members of the ST. James guild
of the Mantle Park Episcopal
church this afternoon at her resi-
dence, on Nineteenth avenue.


=


WEDNESDAY CLUB MEETS.
Mrs. Edith Grosvenor is enter.
tanning the members of the Wednes-
day club this afternoon at the Gros-
venor residence, on Sixth avenue
and Division street.
4 = q


EMERALD CLUB ARE GUESTS


TODAY.


The members Of the Emerald
club are guests today of Mrs. F. T.
Scott at her home, 115 Mansfield
avenue.


WOMAN'S CLUB MEETS.
The millinery department of the
Woman's club met at the club
rooms of the Palace store this
morning at 9 a. m. Miss Ely was
teacher and Mrs. Harry Jarvis was
chairman.


The social economics department
is holding meeting this afternoon
at the library from to A. Mrs.
Rose B. Moore is chairman.
4 & =


PARTY POSTPONED.


The card party arranged by the
Pythian Sisters this afternoon has
been indefinitely postponed.


AA 4


SPOKANE FLORAL ASsoclA.


TON HELD MEETING


TUESDAY.


The members of the Spokane
Floral association held their regu
lar business meeting yesterday aft
ernoon at the apartments of Mrs
w. ellison, in the Kuhn block
The business session was followed
by celebration. of Burbank day
Miss Marie Hornberg expected to
be at the gathering. but was unable
to attend as she had meeting with
the Associated Charities.


An interesting feature of the pro-
gram was the paper on Life of
Luther Burbank by Mrs. N. M. Nc
Leod, and following, Mrs. Archie
Mcleod told in pleasing manner
of the time he devoted to the study
of flowers and human life.


The members planned to go out
to the children's home Thursday
afternoon to see about starting
work on the playground. They also
planned to send the children of the
juvenile court and the ladies of the
old ladies' home candy and colored
Easter eggs for Easter.


The association will also give a
shirt waist party shortly after Lent
at the home of Mrs. +. Millard
Brown. The committee in charge
is composed of Mrs. Brown. Mrs
j. M. Walker and Miss Frances
Burchett.
